SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.physical_plan

Interface RecordBatchReader

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable
All Known Implementing Classes:
SendableRecordBatchStream
public interface RecordBatchReader extends AutoCloseable
Common interface for reading record batches.

This interface is used in two directions:

  • Consuming data from Rust: SendableRecordBatchStream implements this to iterate over query results
  • Providing data to Rust: User implementations provide data for custom ExecutionPlans

The pattern follows Arrow's ArrowReader approach: call getVectorSchemaRoot() once to get the schema and buffer container, then call loadNextBatch() to populate it with each batch of data.

Example: 

try (RecordBatchReader reader = plan.execute(0, taskContext, allocator)) {
    VectorSchemaRoot root = reader.getVectorSchemaRoot();
    while (reader.loadNextBatch()) {
        int rowCount = root.getRowCount();
        // process batch data from root
    }
}
See Also:
  • Rust Arrow: RecordBatch
  • SendableRecordBatchStream

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    close()
    Releases resources held by this reader.
    org.apache.arrow.vector.VectorSchemaRoot
    getVectorSchemaRoot()
    Gets the VectorSchemaRoot that will be populated with data as batches are loaded.
    boolean
    loadNextBatch()
    Loads the next batch of data into the VectorSchemaRoot.

  • Method Details

    • getVectorSchemaRoot

      org.apache.arrow.vector.VectorSchemaRoot getVectorSchemaRoot()
      Gets the VectorSchemaRoot that will be populated with data as batches are loaded.

      The returned VectorSchemaRoot is reused across batches. Each call to loadNextBatch() updates its contents.

      Example: 

      VectorSchemaRoot root = reader.getVectorSchemaRoot();
Schema schema = root.getSchema(); // inspect column metadata
      Returns:
      The VectorSchemaRoot containing the schema and current batch data

    • loadNextBatch

      boolean loadNextBatch()
      Loads the next batch of data into the VectorSchemaRoot.

      After this method returns true, the VectorSchemaRoot from getVectorSchemaRoot() will contain the new batch data. When this method returns false, the stream is exhausted and no more data is available.

      Example: 

      VectorSchemaRoot root = reader.getVectorSchemaRoot();
while (reader.loadNextBatch()) {
    System.out.println("Batch has " + root.getRowCount() + " rows");
}
      Returns:
      true if a batch was loaded, false if no more batches are available
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if loading fails

    • close

      void close()
      Releases resources held by this reader.

      After calling close, the reader should not be used.

      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
